Home / BR-UK Statement
Living document · Coming 2026

BR-UK Statement

Using AI for Behavioural Research Effectively and Responsibly (Coming Soon)

BR-UK is developing a living statement addressing ethical standards, practical benefits and limitations, and recommendations for people doing, using, funding, or commissioning behavioural research. This living document, informed by AI-assisted evidence review, expert consultation and community engagement, will be released in 2026.

Stay informed

We'll announce the statement when it's live. To hear about it first:

  • Join the BR-UK mailing list
  • Tell us what you want the statement to cover via the suggestion form

About BR-UK

The Behavioural Research UK Leadership Hub is supported by the Economic and Social Research Council [Grant Number ES/Y001044/1], comprising partner institutions and government bodies across the UK.

Our contributing institutions include Cardiff University, the University of Edinburgh, the University of Oxford, Queen's University Belfast, the University of Sheffield, the University of St Andrews, University College London — plus government partners across the UK nations.

External behavioural-AI resources

While you wait for our statement, here are a few current, high-quality resources on AI in behavioural research:

  • The Behavioural AI Institute Open Letter
  • AI Evaluation Preprint
  • Queen's University Library Guide
  • LSE AI Research Guidance
  • Caltech Science Exchange
